Web search results for query: "CSS custom properties card density toggle data-density="compact" "comfortable" "spacious" UI pattern"

Links: [{"title":"Designing for Display Density | Bobby Showalter","url":"https://www.bshow.co/fpx/2021/07/08/designing-for-display-density/"},{"title":"Density Toggle Guide - Material React Table V3 Docs","url":"https://www.material-react-table.com/docs/guides/density-toggle"},{"title":"New Density Settings for the Lightning Experience UI in Winter ‘19 | Salesforce Developers Blog","url":"https://developer.salesforce.com/blogs/2018/08/new-density-settings-for-the-lightning-experience-ui-in-winter-19"},{"title":"React Table Block Density Toggle","url":"https://www.shadcn.io/blocks/tables-density"},{"title":"[data grid] How to override grid density factors to change what Compact/Standard/Comfortable means? · Issue #7251 · mui/mui-x","url":"https://github.com/mui/mui-x/issues/7251"},{"title":"Content density - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/foundation/visual-foundation/content-density/"},{"title":"custom properties – CSS-Tricks","url":"https://css-tricks.com/tag/custom-properties/feed/"},{"title":"Display Density · Lightning Design System 2","url":"https://www.lightningdesignsystem.com/2e1ef8501/p/805bbe"},{"title":"CSS Custom Properties Guide | CSS-Tricks","url":"https://css-tricks.com/a-complete-guide-to-custom-properties/"},{"title":"Data table UI design reference guide for 2026 | Setproduct Blog","url":"https://www.setproduct.com/blog/data-table-ui-design"}]

I found comprehensive information about the CSS custom properties card density toggle UI pattern. Here's what the search results reveal:

## Implementation Pattern

The display density is configured as one of three choices: default, compact, or comfortable. A common approach uses data attributes and CSS custom properties:

The implementation uses `[data-ui-density="compact"]` and `[data-ui-density="comfortable"]` selectors to set a `--ui-density` custom property. To write a display density-ready style, developers call the `--ui-density` custom property in a calc function, like `padding: calc(.5em * var(--ui-density))`, and that padding value changes based on the modifier value of `--ui-density`.

## Three-Density Model

The standard pattern supports three density levels: compact, comfortable, and spacious, with instant row height changes via toggle buttons.

## Use Cases and User Preferences

Compact packs the most rows per screen; power users living in the table all day, like analysts and ops teams, almost always want this. Comfortable is the balanced default for mixed audiences with enough breathing room to feel calm and enough density to stay useful.

## Real-World Implementations

This pattern is used across major design systems:
- Some implementations build components at standard display density, then use CSS to calculate "compact" and "comfortable" spacing.
- Cloudscape uses a 4px base unit for the spacing system, where in compact mode, the spacing scale is reduced in increments of 4, decreasing vertical spacing inside components and margins between components.
